answers.text: Fully compatible – The device supports third-party components without restrictions.
Partially compatible – Some third-party components work, but there are limitations (e.g., reduced performance, firmware restrictions).
Limited compatibility – Only certain brands or certified third-party components are supported.
Not compatible – The device only works with manufacturer-approved components.
Data Unavailable– Compatibility is not officially documented.

questions.text: Is the device compatible with third-party data-related components (e.g., firmware chips, hard disk, SSD)?

No – cleaning may cause damage or is restricted

answers.text: All key identification info is clearly visible on the product or in the software.
Partially, some information is visible, but not all (e.g., serial number missing).
It is difficult or impossible to identify the product details.

questions.text: How much complete is the product identification related information (e.g., model, serial number, specifications)?

answers.text: Easy — built-in diagnostics, indicators, or apps clearly show the status.
Moderate — some testing needed, but feasible without special tools.
Difficult — needs expert assessment or proprietary tools to evaluate.
Not possible — no way to assess functionality without dismantling or guessing.
Not applicable – No contracts or transferable ownership rights are associated

questions.text: How easy is it to evaluate the product's core functionality (e.g., what works, what doesn't)?

answers.text: Very easy – Clear visual indicators or diagnostics available
Somewhat easy – Can be assessed with some experience or tools
Difficult – Requires expert knowledge or disassembly
Not possible – No visible signs or tools to assess condition
Not applicable – Product type doesn't show typical wear and tear

questions.text: How easy is it to evaluate the wear and tear of the product?

answers.text: Yes, clearly indicated on the product, documentation, or online
Somewhat accessible — requires effort (e.g., searching by serial number)
No — warranty info is missing or unclear

questions.text: Is warranty-related information easily accessible?

answers.text: Requires following instructions from a manual (multi-step process but user-doable)
Requires a software update or special process (may need external tools or codes)

questions.text: How easy is it to restore the device to physical factory settings?

answers.text: Moderate (Requires a specific sequence of actions)

questions.text: How easy is it to reset the software?

answers.text: Yes – There is a secure and easy way to reset or wipe all personal data
Partially – Some data can be removed, but not all, or the process is complicated
No – There is no clear or secure way to protect previous user data
Not applicable – Product does not store personal data

questions.text: Can the product be reset or prepared in a way that ensures the previous user's personal data is fully deleted and protected before transferring ownership?

answers.text: Yes – Ownership and contracts/warranties can be transferred without issue
Partially – Ownership can be transferred, but contracts or warranties are not transferable
No – Ownership or contractual rights cannot be transferred
Not applicable – No contracts or transferable ownership rights are associated

questions.text: Can ownership of the product, including any associated contracts (e.g. service agreements, warranties), be officially and easily transferred to a new user?
